today_summary: GCM26 last settled $4,167.4 (July 7 session), extending a rally that has added $128.7 over the past five recorded sessions. Max Pain sits at $4,795 — $628 above spot — reflecting a call-heavy chain (P/C OI 0.56, 137,227 calls vs 77,119 puts) anchored by a structural resistance cluster at $5,350-$5,370 (16,010 combined OI, unchanged for a week). Dealer positioning is constructive near spot: net GEX (±20% window) is +$62.9, with the largest nearby positive-gamma pocket at $4,300 (net_gex +$35.5, 3,811 call OI) just $133 above spot. ATM IV has compressed to 22.31% (-1.36pp over five sessions), consistent with a stabilizing long-gamma regime.
five_day_thesis: The dominant five-day story is a GEX regime flip: net gamma ran deeply negative from June 30 (-$177.4) through July 2 (-$60.1), crossed positive on July 3 (+$8.0), and has since built to +$62.9 today — dealers have gone from short-gamma (vol-amplifying) to long-gamma (vol-dampening) as spot rallied $128.7 to $4,167.4. Max pain has barely moved, drifting only -$5 net ($4,800 to $4,795, dipping to $4,780 on July 3) despite the spot rally, because both the $4,000 put wall and $5,370 call wall have sat completely unchanged across all five sessions — the OI skeleton is frozen even as price runs toward it. ATM IV compressed 1.36pp (23.67% to 22.31%) over the same window and the gamma flip strike climbed $100 (from $3,235 to $3,335), both consistent with a market transitioning from volatile, dealer-short-gamma chop into a calmer, dealer-long-gamma grind higher. The put/call OI ratio ticked down (0.59 to 0.56) as call OI grew nearly 7x faster than puts (+9,852 vs +1,441 contracts), reinforcing the bullish positioning shift. Net effect: five sessions ago this chain was primed to accelerate on any dip; today it is primed to dampen moves and pull price toward the $4,250-$4,300 positive-gamma pocket.
what_changed: Net GEX flipped from -$177.4 (Jun 30) to +$62.9 (Jul 7) — the single largest 5-day change, crossing zero on July 3.
Spot rallied $128.7 (+3.2%), from $4,038.7 to $4,167.4, across the window.
ATM IV compressed 1.36pp, from 23.67% to 22.31%, as the positive-gamma regime dampened realized vol.
Put wall held exactly at $4,000 and call wall at $5,370 for all five sessions — zero OI-wall migration despite the spot rally.
Call OI grew 9,852 contracts vs only 1,441 for puts, pulling P/C OI ratio down from 0.59 to 0.56.
Gamma flip strike climbed $100, from $3,235 to $3,335, as the long-gamma zone widened.
prediction: Bias leans constructive above $4,100 — the boundary where local net GEX turns negative (-$10.0) and the long-gamma stabilization breaks down. Above that, the path of least resistance is a grind toward the $4,250-$4,300 positive-gamma pocket (net_gex +$22.5 to +$35.5), the strongest nearby dealer-hedging magnet. A break below $4,100 reopens the prior short-gamma regime and risks a faster move back toward the $4,000 put wall.

day_trade_cta.bias_note: Positive GEX (+$62.9, ±20% window) has held since July 3; bias flips defensive below $4,100 where local gamma turns negative.
day_trade_cta.entry_note: Buy dips into the $4,150 OI node (2,553 calls / 2,501 puts — near pin equilibrium) with the positive-gamma floor still intact above $4,100.
day_trade_cta.pivot_note: Sustained trade above → targets $4,250 then $4,300. Rejection here keeps price rangebound $4,130-$4,200.
day_trade_cta.stop_note: Below the $4,100 strike, local net GEX turns negative (-$10.0) and the vol-dampening regime breaks down.
day_trade_cta.target_note: T1 = $4,250 (call OI 3,019, net_gex +$22.5), take 50% off. T2 = $4,300 (call OI 3,811, net_gex +$35.5), exit remaining.
day_trade_cta.risk_note: ATM IV 22.31% (-1.36pp over 5 sessions) + net GEX +$62.9 → expect a narrower ~1σ daily range ($4,108.8-$4,226.0). Reduce size outside the $4,100-$4,300 gamma-defined channel.
